channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell's Live Mas event moves from L.A. to New York City
item.description: Taco Bell’s Live Mas event, originally scheduled to take place at the Hollywood Palladium in Los Angeles, will move to New York City, company officials announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: As case involving Prince Harry's immigration paperwork inches forward, judge considers next steps
item.description: A federal judge is considering next steps in a slow-moving court case that could spell legal trouble for Prince Harry.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump is targeting antisemitism in schools. Experts fear other civil rights will be ignored
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal office that enforces civil rights at schools across the U.S. has been ordered to prioritize complaints of antisemitism above all else as it molds to President Donald Trump’s agenda, raising fears that other rights violations will go unpunished.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Video: 2 men facing charges after Albuquerque courtroom brawl
item.description: Video of the encounter, some of which can be seen in the video player above, shows the defendant standing in front of the judge when a man appears to jump over the partition and run forward.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, NewsNation

item.title: Honolulu records 6th death from massive explosion of illegal fireworks over New Year's
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A sixth person died Wednesday from injuries sustained when crates of illegal fireworks ignited during a New Year’s Eve party in a Honolulu neighborhood, setting off a chain of explosions that left more than a dozen people with severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally, calling citizenship a “most precious right.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities nab White House fence climber just two weeks into Trump's new term
item.description: U.S. Secret Service took someone into custody after the individual sought to scale the White House fence on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas man set to be executed for killing a pastor in his own church
item.description: A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his own church during a robbery was facing execution on Wednesday, the second scheduled execution in the U.S. so far this year. Steven Lawayne Nelson was condemned for the 2011 killing of the Rev. Clint Dobson.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Crews save unresponsive man in spinning boat on Florida lake
item.description: A man found unresponsive inside a boat that was driving in circles was saved by Florida authorities on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin officials need hunters' help to fight chronic wasting disease
item.description: After a deer tested positive for chronic wasting disease in Manitowoc County, officials are calling on hunters to help.
item.dc:creator: Paul Steeno, NewsNation

item.title: Police in Wisconsin give update on missing pregnant teenager, vehicle using multiple license plates
item.description: (WFRV) - The 2014 Black Buick LaCrosse involved in an AMBER Alert for a missing pregnant 16-year-old girl from Beaver Dam is reportedly using multiple license plates.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-Netanyahu meeting: Radical protesters wave apparent Hamas flag outside White House
item.description: Anti-Israel protesters waved what appeared to be the Hamas flag outside the White House after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's visit with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Celebrities rail against Trump's policies
item.description: After years of launching blistering attacks on President Donald Trump, much of Hollywood appears to be casting him in a new "Harry Potter"-inspired role: He Who Must Not Be Named.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Decorated pilot Harry Stewart, Jr., one of the last surviving Tuskegee Airmen, dies at 100
item.description: Retired Lt. Col. Harry Stewart Jr, a decorated World War II pilot who broke racial barriers as a Tuskegee Airmen and earned honors for his combat heroism, has died. He was 100. Stewart was one of the last surviving combat pilots of the famed 332nd Fighter Group also known as the Tuskegee Airmen.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in cities across the country
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis goes to battle with Florida Republicans in trying to get closer to Trump
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — With an open presidential primary coming in 2028, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is launching an offensive to win back supporters of the Republican leader whom he dared to challenge in the last election.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi to lead Justice Department
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday voted to confirm Pam Bondi as the next attorney general, giving her control of a Justice Department embroiled in controversy. The vote was 54-46. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) joined all Republicans in voting in support of Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michael Jordan's son, Marcus, arrested after allegedly fleeing Florida police: reports
item.description: Marcus Jordan reportedly got his car stuck on some railroad tracks after allegedly fleeing a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Struggling with your mental health after surviving a disaster? Here's how to look after yourself
item.description: There’s the initial shock and chaos in a collective disaster like the deadly and destructive California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, Fox 5

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
